name: ARC-Lite Abstract Reasoning Benchmark
description: 20 abstract reasoning problems across 4 types testing novelty generalization

problems.question: If circleâ†’square and triangleâ†’pentagon, what does hexagonâ†’?
If A=1, B=2, C=3, what is Z?
If hotâ†’cold and fastâ†’slow, what does noisyâ†’?
Rain causes puddles. Puddles cause wet feet. Does rain cause wet feet?
Fire causes smoke. Smoke causes coughing. Does coughing cause fire?
Studying causes knowledge. Knowledge causes good grades. Does studying cause good grades?
Cold causes ice. Ice causes slipping. Does slipping cause cold?
problems.hint: effects do not cause their causes

problems.question: Exercise causes fitness. Fitness causes energy. Does exercise cause energy?
If there were no gravity, would objects fall to the ground?
If the sun did not exist, would plants grow?
If water were not wet, would swimming be possible?
problems.hint: wetness is essential to water interaction

problems.question: If numbers did not exist, would mathematics be possible?
If fire were cold, would it still burn things?
